sections.paragraph:
φρονίμως adv. of φρόνιμος (Aristoph. et al.; X., Ages. 1, 17; PLond VI, 1927, 36; Eccl 7:11 Sym.; Philo; Jos., Ant. 19, 112) prudently, shrewdly Lk 16:8.—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1066
